item.title: the only rule that matters is the golden rule
item.description: Context: The penguins were stuck in a dip in the snow/ice with no way out. The crew broke it to save the penguins from freezing to death.

item.title: Going, going, Gone.
item.description: A Cybertr, erm, WankPanzer has sunk in the harbor in Ventura, Calif. after its driver attempted to load a jet ski into the water. Musk previously claimed the WankPanzer could be used as a boat for a short period of time, allowing the vehicle to cross rivers.

item.title: I made a video of a teriyaki bottle going through an existential crisis
item.description:
Why would you make this poor teriyaki bottle go through an existential crisis? Good question. Basically I saw a creative contest for a trip to Japan that literally just said “make anything creative using this Teriyaki bottle”. Super vague requirements so naturally I made a video about the teriyaki bottle going through an existential crisis and he just wants to go back home to Japan to see his family.
Thought you guys might think it’s funny. Let me know what you think. Does it make sense? I was trying to go for the “early 2000s Chef Boyardee can comes to life and chases after little girl” vibe.
I made him a little hobo bindle with a chopstick, as one does when running away.
I made it into the top 6 so fingers crossed. Hopefully he gets his happy ending in Japan…wait no, I mean with his family. FP Edit: Appreciate you guys! I’m glad the story comes through. If anyone wants to help vote for my video I’d really appreciate it but no pressure: www.kikkomanteriyakithatchallenge.com/vote
item.dc:creator: ItsMoni

item.title: Needless to say, the family dinner was awkward.
item.description: My siblings (much older than me) are very lucky that they have been able to stay in the same companies and move consistently upwards. That is not normal these days. At least in Europe you get things like benefits, public transportation (usually) and vacation days. I was astonished that my current job gave me 96 hours (12 days) of PTO a year as it was almost double my previous job and the other offer I received. They responded about needing to pay US & European taxes. “Well that’s only if I remain a US citizen, which I am seeing less and less reason to.”
item.dc:creator: Kairuf

item.title: Is this a "green flag"?
item.description: Is this what one should look for in a man? Is this what secure masculinity looks like? I should sleep on this. Edit: I did not expect this to blow up, but I'm glad you lot enjoyed it. Just to be clear, I'm pretty sure the guy in the video was being humorous and wasn't actually expecting it to escalate. He simply processed the situation well and is clearly secure in himself. I woke up realising my last two relationships (the longest) have been with men who have fragile egos. It's going to take time to unlearn a lot of that from this past decade. Or I could figure out how to get involved in the current hellstorm of this political climate and contribute to the betterment of society rather than feed my selfish need for affection. I've also never asked for anything on my MV posts, but let's see if this works... pls send me pics of your pups/kitties + their breeds, and/or names (pics, if you oblige) of your favourite sweets, so that I can try recreating them in a more nutritious form (without white carbs and processed sugars).
item.dc:creator: unwaver

item.dc:creator: cunninglinguist85

item.title: Tolkien lore – human sorcerers and the black arts
item.description:
This is a lore account on the mortals who used sorcery, and what sorcery may contain?
What Men and Hobbits called magic was to Tolkien abilities innate to users own nature; the use of 'magic' in his legendarium "is not to be come by 'lore' or spells; but is in an inherent power not possessed or attainable by Men as such" (Letter 155, art by Tristan Wang).
Overt magic wielders were incarnated spirits, as Sauron and the Wizards, or Elves who had abilities or capacities beyond those of mankind [including hobbits]. Mankind did not utterly lack capacity for such things as telepaty [sanwe-latya] or foresight about things to come, but it was rare that it was powerful or that they gained any kind of control over the potential, in difference to the Eldar (art by Brothers Hildebrandt).
One human people differed somewhat: the mighty Dúnedain, who originated on the island of Númenor in the Second Age. The Númenóreans made colonies in Middle-earth where, except for in Arnor and Gondor, they came to worship the Dark Lord Sauron: "Many became enamoured of the Darkness and the black arts;" – The Two Towers; IV; V (art by MrSvein872)
Sometime after 1695 of the Second Age Sauron handed out nine Great Rings to Men, of which three were Númenóreans (Silmarillion; V): "Those who used the Nine Rings became mighty in their day, kings, sorcerers, and warriors of old. They obtained glory and great wealth, yet it turned to their undoing." If any of the sorcerers were known as such before they got a Ring - that offered them invisibility, enhanced inherent powers, and prolonged life - is not known (art by spartank42).
The kingdom of Arnor broke into three realms in year 861 of the Third Age [yellow, green and red]. In 1300 the Witch-king, lord of the nine Ringwraiths and at least as a Nazgûl was a verified user of magic, established the evil kingdom of Angmar [blue]. His mission was to destroy the Northern Dúnedain and he succeeded in the realm of Rhudaur [red] already in 1409;
"In Rhudaur an evil folk, workers of sorcery, subjects of Angmar, slay the remnants of the Dunedain and build dark forts in the hills." – History of Middle-Earth 12; VII "1409 ... in Rhudaur for long there dwelt an evil people out of the North, much given to sorcery." – HoME 12; VIII (art by merlkir)
In year 3019 Gandalf and the Host of the West met the emissary from Mordor at the Black Gate. Known as The Mouth of Sauron he had a monstrous horse but [in difference to in the 2003 film] was himself a plain human in apperance (art by Ted Nasmith). The Mouth of Sauron belonged to those Dúnedain who were corrupted in the Second Age, or heirs of mingled blood, known as the Black Númenóreans;
"for they established their dwellings in Middle-earth during the years of Sauron’s domination, and they worshipped him, being enamoured of evil knowledge. And he entered the service of the Dark Tower when it first rose again, and because of his cunning he grew ever higher in the Lord’s favour; and he learned great sorcery, and knew much of the mind of Sauron; and he was more cruel than any orc." – RotK; V; X (art by Douglas Beekman)
So what is this sorcery, these black arts, that wicked humans engaged in? We do not know, but I shall lay out some possible answers rooted in lore - though I stress that none of them are described as being it, or part of it. To Tolkien magic in general was neutral and merely used for good or for evil (Letter 155); still black arts must have included something. It is told how even in Gondor old lords of the Dúnedain , in desire to prolong their lives, in secret "compounded strong elixirs, or in high cold towers asked questions of the stars" (TTT; IV; V). Perhaps such fields of study could been turned to evil use?
As told Men had the capacity for sanwe-latya [telepathy]. It was not on par with that of spirits or Quendi [Elves] who had better control of their hröa [body] that, together with vocal speech (!), could weaken sanwe-latya (The Nature of Middle-earth; II; IX): "Men have the same faculty as the Quendi, but it is in itself weaker, and is weaker in operation owing to the strength of the hröa, over which most men have small control by the will." Telepathy allowed communication but also to read things in the minds of others, lest they were unwilling. All this was beyond most Men but a case for that a few could is Steward Denethor.
Denethor II of Gondor had a strong Númenórean heritage and Gandalf said (RotK; V; I, art by peet): "He has long sight. He can perceive, if he bends his will thither, much of what is passing in the minds of men, even of those that dwell far off. It is difficult to deceive him, and dangerous to try." Dúnedain were rumoured to be able to understand animals. At least the cruel Black Númenórean Beruthiel, who was Queen of Gondor for a period, could converse with her cats and read their minds, and use them as spies (Unfinished Tales; IV; II; Note 7).
A different matter is shape-changing. Spirits in their origin could change form, as often deployed by Sauron and the Wizard Radgast was a "master of shapes and changes of hue" (FotR; II; II). Elves knew arts by which the magic wielder and allies could take on the shape and form of slain foes, whether Orc or beast (Silmarillion; III; XIX). Some humans could transform to animals: "Though a skin-changer and no doubt a bit of a magician, Beorn was a Man" (Letter 144). Beorn turning into a bear was due to "no enchantment but his own" and "for many generations the men of his line had the power of taking bear’s shape" (The Hobbit; VII + XVIII).
Another remarkable skill was that the Dúnedain once crafted enchanted weapons. The daggers found in the burial mounds of the Barrow Downs had been crafted by smiths in Arnor, "foes of the Dark Lord", and its potency was best seen when Merry stabbed the Witch-king that broke "the spell that knit his unseen sinews to his will", whence Lady Éowyn killed him (FotR; I; VIII + RotK; V; VI, art by Alexander Mokhov). Yet there are no other enchanted arms or tools made by humans [excluding the Woses and their statues that is a topic apart] so it must been a very rare indeed.
These various supernatural abilities mentioned are not an exhaustive list, but the most credible answer to what is covered by dark arts is necromancy. Sauron learned it long ago from his former master Morgoth, became a master of it and as shall be seen taught it to others. Not for not was Sauron known as the Necromancer when he was in Mirkwood.
Note this is necromancy in the Biblical sense of interacting with dead spirits, not the broader modern fantasy concept of raising undead bodies or casting harmful spells (art: summing the spirit of the prophet Samuel by William Sidney Mount). Except for the Oathbreakers, the undead spirits of concern in Middle-earth were not human but those of Elves who lingered behind.
Slain Elves who in some manner had been corrupted (by unfullfilled desires for example) could refuse to go to the Halls of the Dead on the other side of the Sea. They remained in Middle-earth and are called unbodied or houseless spirits: "Not all of these are kindly or unstained by the Shadow." – HoME 10; Laws and Customs among the Eldar; Of Re-birth and Other Dooms' onward
"It is therefore a foolish and perilous thing, besides being a wrong deed... if the Living seek to commune with the Unbodied, though the houseless may desire it, especially the most unworthy among them. For the Unbodied, wandering in the world, are those who at the least have refused the door of life and remain in regret and self-pity. Some are filled with bitterness, grievance, and envy" (art by Khang Le).
"Some were enslaved by the Dark Lord and do his work still, though he himself is gone. They will not speak truth or wisdom. To call on them is folly. To attempt to master them and to make them servants of one own's will is wickedness. Such practices are of Morgoth; and the necromancers are of the host of Sauron his servant."
"Some say that the Houseless desire bodies... The wicked among them will take bodies, if they can, unlawfully. The peril of communing with them is, therefore, not only the peril of being deluded by fantasies or lies: there is peril also of destruction." When 'ghosts' tried to take over a body of a living the body could be gravely injured, even if it was not wrested from its rightful inhabitant (art by Toru-meow).
"Or the Houseless may plead for shelter, and if it is admitted, then it will seek to enslave its host and use both his will and his body for its own purposes. It is said that Sauron did these things, and taught his followers how to achieve them."
An example of this were the Barrow wights in the burial mounds of Barrow Downs. The wights had been sent there by the Witch-king after 1409 (art by John Howe). It also is plausible that the werewolves serving Sauron were created through necromancy, for they were "fell beasts inhabited by dreadful spirits that he had imprisoned in their bodies" (Silmarillion; III; XIX).
I do not find support for humans being able to command or bind spirits as the Witch-king could, nor force any to possess beasts as Sauron with the werewolves, yet perhaps managed less powerful necromantic sorcery (art by Jan Drenovec). At end, until new sources emerge we are left to speculations. Tolkien often presented his stories as surviving records from writers who did not wield magic themselves and so left matters vague, and this 'realism' merely results in unanswered questions which stir the imagination.
Today is the six-year anniversary of my first Tolkien lore post on imgur. It has been a pleasure and a honour to have had your time, encouragements, questions, and commendations. Thank you!
Some related accounts: Queen Beruthiel https://imgur.com/gallery/9Fem6Au The Barrow-downs https://imgur.com/gallery/U5oQvWy The Dead Marshes https://imgur.com/gallery/Rbw0jY9 Magic of the Dwarves https://imgur.com/gallery/8IAZpoH – – – – INDEX: character bios https://imgur.com/gallery/axV2mPF INDEX: folks, animals, items, etc https://imgur.com/gallery/AQK9pJL

item.title: The fascist Elon "Mushk for brains" is probably destroying the ozone layer
item.description: Turns out mega constellations of short lived satellites is a bad thing. It truly hurts, as I work in this business. Nothing compared to starlink, but we are still a part of it. One of our greatest feats was repairing the ozone layer. Please read: https://agupubs.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1029/2024GL109280
item.dc:creator: oxyacetyleneballoon

item.title: Scoots and the shoe
item.description:
Scooty Booty has taken a shine to my tennis shoes. I went to put them on today to work out and the laces had all been pulled apart. Now I know why!! What an adorable little weirdo.
I don’t think it fits, Scoots.

item.dc:creator: OceansRust

item.title: GNU Terry Pratchett
item.description: The nature of grief can be strange. Today is the 10 year anniversary of the death of Terry Pratchett, a man whom I never met, never interacted with beyond consuming his books over and over and over since I was barely old enough to read them. And yet I mourn him, I genuinely miss him being in this world and feel a depth of sadness that surprises me. The world is a worse place without him, so, in his honour: Be funny, be kind, be a voice for those who cannot speak, stand up for those who need you, and be absolutely furiously angry about the injustice in the world. GNU Terry Pratchett.
item.dc:creator: VinnieJonesDiary

item.title: Now they tell me
item.description: Cornstarch Solution Ratio: 1 part cornstarch to 2 parts water (e.g., 1 tablespoon cornstarch to 2 tablespoons water). Why: Cornstarch is a strong thickener and forms a gel-like consistency when mixed with water and heated. This ratio creates a slurry that’s pourable but thickens quickly when stirred into warm oil, helping it grab onto food particles. How to Use: Mix the cornstarch and cold water until smooth, then pour it into the used oil (still warm, around 120–150°F or 50–65°C) while stirring. The heat will activate the starch, forming a clump with the food bits.
item.dc:creator: LosPer

item.title: A pose a day: Princess Rowena sitting
item.description: Hi! I'm AdorkaStock. I photograph myself and others in various poses for art reference. My goal is to include in my galleries of stock poses a variety of body types, skin types, and people doing various things so there is a piece of stock for everyone. I want to share my stock, which is free to use for reference, with as many people as possible in case they need it! FAQ is in my bio. Hope this pose helps you! Happy drawing! #Art #AdorkaStock #Reference #Artist
item.dc:creator: adorkastock

item.title: Of course
item.description: I was JUST about to stand up and make my dinner, and Scooty Booty jumped up and started grooming herself and getting comfortable in my lap. Since I’ve been back in the office full-time, she doesn’t spend much time in my lap. I’m obviously not here as much, and I don’t have the laptop here that she can snuggle underneath, and she resents that. Now I’ve either gotta sit here until she moves or move her myself and feel like a terrible person. Freaking Scoots
item.dc:creator: KittensForDays
